meta.description: mostlyCBD Evidence Library â€” curated cannabis research. Scored, tagged, and human-reviewed before publish.

studies.title: CBD: A Systematic Review of Clinical and Preclinical Evidence in the Treatment of Pain
studies.authors: Multiple authors, MDPI Pharmaceuticals
studies.plain_summary: PRISMA-guided review of 40 studies on CBD for pain. Found consistent analgesic properties across neuropathic, inflammatory, and chronic pain â€” with no significant adverse effects reported in clinical or preclinical trials. Notes activation of 5HT-1A receptors as a likely mechanism.
studies.score_notes: Systematic review following PRISMA, peer-reviewed, recent (2024), broad scope, no conflicts reported

studies.title: Are Cannabis-Based Medicines a Useful Treatment for Neuropathic Pain? A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Almuntashiri N, El Sharazly BM, Carter WG â€” University of Nottingham
studies.plain_summary: Reviewed 22 RCTs from 2003â€“2024 on cannabis-based medicines for neuropathic pain. Found promising evidence for balanced THC:CBD formulations (nabiximols/Sativex), with low risk of bias in most studies. Notes regulatory barriers as a major obstacle to expanded research.
studies.score_notes: Fresh (2025), systematic review of RCTs, dual risk-of-bias review, peer-reviewed journal, no conflicts

studies.title: Cannabis-Based Products for Chronic Pain: An Updated Systematic Review
studies.authors: Chou R, Fu R, Ahmed AY, Morasco BJ â€” Oregon Health & Science University
studies.plain_summary: 25 RCTs (n=2,303; 64% neuropathic pain). Comparable THC:CBD ratio products probably reduce pain severity with small effect sizes. High-THC-only products showed increased adverse events. Calls for long-term outcome studies. AHRQ-funded â€” no industry conflicts.
studies.score_notes: Annals of Internal Medicine (top-tier), AHRQ-funded (no industry bias), 2026 publication, PROSPERO registered, dual extraction

studies.title: Effectiveness of Cannabidiol to Manage Chronic Pain: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Multiple authors, PRISMA 2020
studies.journal: Journal of Pain & Palliative Care Pharmacotherapy
studies.plain_summary: 15 studies included from 8 databases. Majority showed pain reduction of 42â€“66% with CBD. Three studies showed no significant improvement. Concludes CBD may be useful for chronic pain but calls for more robust designs and standardized dosing protocols.
studies.score_notes: PRISMA-guided, multi-database, no conflicts, moderate heterogeneity across included studies

studies.title: Therapeutic Potential of Cannabidiol (CBD) in Anxiety Disorders: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
studies.authors: Multiple authors
studies.journal: European Journal of Medicinal Chemistry Reports
studies.plain_summary: Meta-analysis of 8 eligible studies (n=316). CBD showed a substantial effect on anxiety (Hedges' g = -0.92). 70% of highest-quality studies reported positive outcomes for GAD, SAD, and PTSD. 30% showed negative results for OCD, trichotillomania, and test anxiety. Calls for standardized dosing and longitudinal research.
studies.score_notes: Meta-analysis with effect size calculation, recent (2024), peer-reviewed, notes limitations honestly

studies.title: Evidence for Use of Cannabinoids in Mood Disorders, Anxiety Disorders, and PTSD: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Stanciu CN, Brunette MF, Teja N, Budney AJ â€” Dartmouth
studies.plain_summary: Only 8 controlled trials met inclusion criteria. Found insufficient evidence for CBD or THC to manage anxiety, mood disorders, or PTSD. Single-dose CBD showed acute anxiety reduction. High-dose THC in depressed patients worsened symptoms in >50%. Important counterweight â€” the evidence base is still thin.
studies.score_notes: Dartmouth, Psychiatric Services (peer-reviewed, clinical), honest negative finding, no conflicts

studies.title: Cannabidiol in Anxiety and Sleep: A Large Case Series
studies.authors: Shannon S, Lewis N, Lee H, Hughes S
studies.plain_summary: 72-patient chart review at a psychiatric clinic. Anxiety scores decreased in 79.2% within the first month and remained decreased. Sleep improved in 66.7% but fluctuated over time. CBD was well tolerated in all but 3 patients. Observational design â€” no control group.
studies.score_notes: Real-world clinical data, peer-reviewed, honest about limitations; docked for lack of control group

studies.title: Cannabidiol as a Potential Treatment for Anxiety Disorders
studies.authors: Blessing EM, Steenkamp MM, Manzanares J, Marmar CR
studies.plain_summary: Foundational review of preclinical and clinical evidence. Demonstrates CBD's efficacy across GAD, panic disorder, SAD, OCD, and PTSD in animal models. Human data support anxiolytic effects with no anxiogenic properties and excellent safety profile. Notes all human studies used acute dosing â€” chronic dosing data lacking.
studies.score_notes: Neurotherapeutics (high-impact), foundational paper, NYU authors, docked slightly for age (2015) and limited chronic dosing data

studies.title: Clinical and Cognitive Improvement Following Full-Spectrum, High-CBD Treatment for Anxiety: Open-Label Phase 2 Trial
studies.authors: Dahlgren MK et al.
studies.plain_summary: 14 outpatients with moderate-to-severe anxiety treated with 4 weeks of full-spectrum high-CBD sublingual solution (9.97 mg/mL CBD, 0.23 mg/mL THC). Significant anxiety reduction on primary outcomes. Improvements also in mood, sleep, quality of life, and executive function. No serious adverse events.
studies.score_notes: Nature Portfolio journal, full-spectrum (real-world relevant), docked for small n=14 and open-label design

studies.title: Cannabis, Pain, and Sleep: Lessons from Therapeutic Clinical Trials of Sativex
studies.authors: Russo EB
studies.plain_summary: Reviews Phase Iâ€“III Sativex (1:1 THC:CBD) trials across 2,000 subjects with 1,000 patient-years exposure. Marked improvement in subjective sleep in patients with neuropathic pain, MS, cancer pain, and rheumatoid arthritis. CBD has mild activating effect; THC-dominant extracts show residual sedation. Foundational balanced-ratio data.
studies.score_notes: Ethan Russo (respected cannabinoid researcher), massive clinical dataset, docked for age (2007) and Sativex industry connection

studies.title: The Interplay between the Endocannabinoid System, Epilepsy, and Cannabinoids
studies.authors: Cheung KAK, Peiris H, Wallace G, Holland OJ, Mitchell MD
studies.plain_summary: Examines how the ECS regulates neuroinflammation and seizure activity, and how cannabinoids may reduce incidence and severity of epileptic events. CBD highlighted as most promising therapeutic cannabinoid due to non-psychoactive profile and neuroprotective properties. Notes lack of rigorous long-term safety data.
studies.score_notes: Open access, clear mechanism review, peer-reviewed MDPI, docked for age and review-only design

studies.title: Targeting Endocannabinoid System in Epilepsy: For Good or for Bad
studies.authors: MartÃ­nez-Aguirre C, Cinar R, Rocha L â€” Center for Research and Advanced Studies, Mexico City
studies.plain_summary: Presents a nuanced view: ECS activation can both suppress AND facilitate seizure activity depending on conditions. CB1 signaling can promote neuroinflammation under certain pathological conditions. Important counterbalance to purely positive framings of cannabinoid use in epilepsy.
studies.score_notes: Elsevier Neuroscience, honest mixed finding, important counterpoint, no conflicts

studies.title: Oral Cannabis for Taxane-Induced Neuropathy: A Pilot Randomized Placebo-Controlled Study
studies.authors: Haney M, Choo T, Tiersten A et al. â€” Columbia University
studies.plain_summary: 8-week double-blind RCT in breast cancer patients with taxane-induced neuropathy. Both groups improved over time, but cannabis patients had significantly higher neuropathy ratings and lower functional well-being in final weeks vs placebo. A carefully conducted negative trial â€” rare and valuable.
studies.score_notes: Columbia University, double-blind RCT design, 2025, rigorous â€” negative results are essential to include

studies.title: Cannabidiol: A Potential New Alternative for the Treatment of Anxiety, Depression, and Psychotic Disorders
studies.authors: GarcÃ­a-GutiÃ©rrez MS et al.
studies.plain_summary: Reviews animal and human data for CBD's anxiolytic, antidepressant, and antipsychotic potential. Effects are dose- and route-dependent. Key targets include CB1/CB2 receptors, 5-HT1A receptors, and neurogenesis factors. Preliminary clinical trials show positive risk-benefit profile. Calls for large-scale RCTs.
studies.score_notes: Frontiers in Psychiatry, open access, honest about evidence gaps, no conflicts

studies.title: ALS, the Endocannabinoid System, and Exogenous Cannabinoids: Current State and Clinical Implications
studies.authors: Denton TT, Carter GT, Goddard M et al. â€” Washington State University
studies.plain_summary: Reviews preclinical and emerging clinical evidence for cannabinoids in ALS. THC, CBD, CBG, and other cannabinoids show antioxidative, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties in SOD1 mouse models, including delayed disease onset and prolonged neuronal survival. Human clinical data remains limited.
studies.score_notes: WSU/Steve Gleason Institute, Muscle & Nerve journal (respected), 2025, Ethan Russo co-author

studies.title: Cannabidiol Modulates Serotonergic Transmission and Reverses Both Allodynia and Anxiety-like Behavior in Neuropathic Pain
studies.authors: De Gregorio D, McLaughlin RJ, Posa L et al.
studies.plain_summary: Preclinical study demonstrating CBD reverses allodynia (pain from non-painful stimuli) and anxiety in a neuropathic pain model via 5-HT1A serotonin receptor activation. Directly relevant to patients who experience co-occurring neuropathic pain and anxiety â€” suggests a unified mechanism.
studies.score_notes: Published in PAIN (top-tier pain journal), important mechanism discovery, docked for preclinical-only design

studies.title: Oral capsules of tetra-hydro-cannabinol (THC), cannabidiol (CBD) and their combination in peripheral neuropathic pain treatment
studies.authors: Zubcevic K, Petersen M, Bach FW et al.
studies.score_notes: Study type: randomized (+18); Journal: Eur J Pain (+12); Recency: 2023, 3 years old (+10); CBD relevance: 3 keywords (+12); Sample size signals (+8)
